title: brassica rapa var. rosularis
intro: Brassica rapa var. rosularis, also known as rosary cabbage or rosularis, is a cool-season annual crop belonging to the Brassicaceae family, a group of plants that includes broccoli, cauliflower, and kale. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its high content of glucosinolates, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of rosary cabbage include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal medicine. Rosary cabbage can be prepared in various herbal forms, including fresh leaves, steamed or sautéed as a vegetable, or infused as a tea to utilize its medicinal properties.
